moonPage.description: Live lunar phase with illumination and monthly calendar. New and full moon dates calculated with Swiss Ephemeris to ±1 minute accuracy.
moonPage.relatedWhy: Why sidereal astrology differs from tropical
moonPage.faq1Q: How is moon phase illumination calculated?
moonPage.faq1A: Illumination comes from the Sun–Moon elongation angle: (1 − cos θ) / 2 × 100%. At New Moon (0°) illumination is 0%; at Full Moon (180°) it is 100%.
moonPage.faq2Q: Sidereal vs tropical — does the moon phase change?
moonPage.faq2A: No. Moon phase is the angular gap between the Sun and Moon — identical in both systems. The ~24° Lahiri ayanamsa offset shifts the Moon's zodiac sign but not its phase angle.
moonPage.faq3Q: How accurate are the new and full moon dates?
moonPage.faq3A: Dates are calculated with Swiss Ephemeris using the Moshier analytical ephemeris, accurate to ±0.01°. The event time is narrowed by binary search to within ±1 minute.

hoursPage.description: Traditional Chaldean hours for your location. Each hour carries the energy of its ruling planet — use them to time actions with intention.
hoursPage.faq1A: Planetary hours are an ancient Chaldean timekeeping system dividing each day and night into 12 unequal segments, each governed by one of the seven classical planets — Sun, Moon, Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Venus, Saturn. The ruling planet changes every hour and influences the energy of that time period.
hoursPage.faq2A: The day is split into 12 equal parts between sunrise and sunset (day hours) and 12 equal parts between sunset and the next sunrise (night hours). Hour length varies by season and latitude. The first hour of each day is ruled by the weekday planet — Sun on Sunday, Moon on Monday, Mars on Tuesday, Mercury on Wednesday, Jupiter on Thursday, Venus on Friday, Saturn on Saturday.
hoursPage.faq3Q: Sidereal vs tropical — does it matter?
hoursPage.faq3A: Planetary hours are based on sunrise/sunset times, not zodiac positions, so they are identical in both sidereal and tropical astrology. The Chaldean sequence (Saturn, Jupiter, Mars, Sun, Venus, Mercury, Moon) is independent of ayanamsa.

essaysPage.description: In sidereal astrology, planetary positions are calculated against the fixed stars using the Lahiri ayanamsa — roughly 24° earlier than the tropical zodiac most Western horoscopes use. Below are 120 interpretations: each of the ten classical planets placed in each of the twelve signs of the sidereal zodiac.

whySidereal.h1: Why Sidereal Astrology Differs from Tropical
whySidereal.leadParagraph: Sidereal astrology measures zodiac positions against the actual constellations in the sky, correcting for the precession of the equinoxes using the Lahiri ayanamsa (currently ~24°07′). Tropical astrology — used by most Western horoscopes — anchors Aries to the spring equinox, which has drifted ~24 degrees from the Aries constellation over 2,000 years. The practical result: most people's sidereal sun sign is one sign earlier than their tropical sign, and all planetary positions shift by roughly 24 days.
whySidereal.section1P1: Earth's rotational axis is not fixed — it traces a slow circle in the sky over approximately 25,772 years, like a spinning top gradually wobbling. This motion, called the precession of the equinoxes, causes the spring equinox point to drift westward through the zodiac constellations at approximately 50.3 arc-seconds per year — about 1 degree every 72 years.
whySidereal.section1P2: When the Western zodiac was codified (approximately 2,000 years ago in ancient Greece), the spring equinox aligned closely with the Aries constellation. Tropical astrology froze that alignment: 0° Aries always equals the spring equinox. Sidereal astrology did not freeze it. It tracks where the Aries constellation actually is today — which is now approximately 24°07′ behind where it was when the tropical system was created.
whySidereal.section1P3: The International Astronomical Union (IAU) defines the constellation boundaries used as sidereal reference points. The Lahiri ayanamsa — adopted as the Indian national standard in 1955 — calculates this drift with precision, making it the most rigorously defined sidereal reference in use today.
whySidereal.section2Heading: How Sidereal and Tropical Differ in Practice
whySidereal.section2P1: The practical consequence is that if your tropical horoscope says you are a Scorpio, your sidereal chart likely shows your Sun in Libra — or Scorpio if you were born in the second half of Scorpio's tropical window. Every planet in your chart shifts by approximately 24°07′, which for most people moves each planet into the previous zodiac sign.
whySidereal.section2P2: This is not a correction of an error in tropical astrology — it is a different measurement framework asking different questions. Tropical astrology models the relationship between humanity and the solar seasons; sidereal astrology models the relationship between humanity and the fixed star background. Both descriptions have interpretive validity; they simply describe different layers of the astronomical situation at birth.
whySidereal.section3Heading: Sidereal vs Tropical Dates for All 12 Signs
whySidereal.section3IntroBefore: Approximate dates for 2026. Exact boundaries shift slightly each year due to the continuing precession of the equinoxes (~50.3″/year) and variations in planetary positions. Use the
whySidereal.datesTableAria: Sidereal and tropical zodiac sign dates comparison 2026
whySidereal.section4Heading: The Lahiri Ayanamsa: Estrevia's Calculation Standard
whySidereal.section4P1End: = part) is the angular correction applied to convert a tropical position to a sidereal one. As of 2026, the Lahiri ayanamsa is approximately 24°07′ — meaning every planet's sidereal longitude is 24°07′ less than its tropical longitude.
whySidereal.section4P2Before: Estrevia calculates all positions using the
whySidereal.section4P2After: (accuracy ±0.01°) with the Lahiri ayanamsa mode (SE_SIDM_LAHIRI = 1). The Swiss Ephemeris is the same calculation engine used by Astro.com and most professional astrology software, ensuring that Estrevia's results can be verified against any other serious sidereal tool.
whySidereal.section4P3: Multiple ayanamsa systems exist — Fagan-Bradley (popular in Western sidereal astrology), Krishnamurti (used in KP astrology), and others. They differ by fractions of a degree. Lahiri is the most widely used globally and the official standard of the Government of India. Estrevia uses Lahiri exclusively in MVP; Fagan-Bradley and Krishnamurti support are planned for Phase 2.
whySidereal.ctaP: Enter your birth date, time, and location. Estrevia calculates your Sun, Moon, Ascendant, and all 10 planetary positions using the Lahiri ayanamsa and Swiss Ephemeris (±0.01° accuracy).
whySidereal.faq1A: Sidereal astrology is a zodiac system that measures planetary positions against the actual constellations in the sky. It uses the Lahiri ayanamsa — currently approximately 24°07′ — to correct for the precession of the equinoxes. Most sidereal practitioners align with Vedic (Jyotish) tradition. The result is that sidereal zodiac dates are roughly 24 days later than tropical dates.
whySidereal.faq2Q: What is the difference between sidereal and tropical astrology?
whySidereal.faq2A: Tropical astrology fixes Aries at the spring equinox (around March 21), regardless of where the Aries constellation actually is. Sidereal astrology tracks the actual position of the Aries constellation. Due to the precession of the equinoxes — a 26,000-year wobble in Earth's axis — the two systems have drifted approximately 24 degrees apart. This means most people's sidereal sun sign is one sign earlier than their tropical sun sign.
whySidereal.faq3A: The ayanamsa is the angular difference between the tropical and sidereal zodiacs at any given moment. The Lahiri ayanamsa — also called the Chitrapaksha ayanamsa — is the official standard of the Indian government and the most widely used in Vedic astrology. As of 2026, it is approximately 24°07′. Estrevia uses the Lahiri ayanamsa for all calculations via the Swiss Ephemeris library.
whySidereal.faq4Q: Will my zodiac sign change with sidereal astrology?
whySidereal.faq4A: For most people, yes. If you were born in the first 24 days of your tropical zodiac sign, your sidereal sun sign is likely the previous sign. For example, an Aries born April 1 (tropical) is a sidereal Pisces. Someone born April 15 is a sidereal Aries. Calculate your chart to find your actual sidereal positions.
whySidereal.faq5Q: What is the precession of the equinoxes?
whySidereal.faq5A: The precession of the equinoxes is a 25,772-year cycle caused by the gravitational pull of the Moon and Sun on Earth's equatorial bulge. It causes Earth's rotational axis to trace a slow circle in the sky — much like a spinning top slowly wobbling. The result is that the spring equinox point drifts westward through the zodiac constellations at approximately 50.3 arc-seconds per year, or about 1 degree every 72 years.
whySidereal.faq6Q: Is sidereal astrology more accurate than tropical?
whySidereal.faq6A: Accuracy depends on what you are measuring. Sidereal astrology is astronomically accurate — it describes where planets actually are relative to the constellations. Tropical astrology is seasonally accurate — it connects zodiac positions to the solar year and seasons. Neither is objectively more "correct"; they measure different things. Many practitioners, particularly those trained in Vedic tradition, argue that sidereal placement is more personally accurate for individual natal charts.
whySidereal.faq7Q: Which planets does sidereal astrology apply to?
whySidereal.faq7A: Sidereal astrology shifts the zodiac position of all celestial bodies: Sun, Moon, Mercury, Venus, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune, Pluto, North Node (Rahu), and Chiron. The ayanamsa correction applies equally to all planets — each one's zodiac position shifts by approximately 24°07′ relative to its tropical position.

landing.statsHeading: Join astrologers discovering their sidereal signs
landing.statsSubtitle: Most discover they carry the energy of the sign before their tropical Sun — not the one on their horoscope app.
landing.faq1Q: What is the difference between sidereal and tropical astrology?
landing.faq1A: Tropical astrology fixes the zodiac to the equinoxes — the Sun enters Aries on the spring equinox. Sidereal astrology aligns the zodiac to the actual star constellations. Due to the precession of the equinoxes (~50″ per year), the two systems are currently ~24° apart. Most people in sidereal astrology find their Sun is in the sign before their tropical one.
landing.faq2A: The ayanamsa is the angular offset between tropical and sidereal zodiacs. Lahiri (also called Chitrapaksha) is the official ayanamsa adopted by the Indian government and the most widely used in Vedic/Jyotish astrology. As of 2026 it is approximately 24°7′.
landing.faq3A: Estrevia uses Swiss Ephemeris — the same engine used by Solar Fire and Astro.com — with ±0.01° accuracy for all 12 celestial bodies. Over 100 reference charts are verified in CI against Astro.com before every release.
landing.faq4A: A shareable card with your sidereal Sun, Moon, and Ascendant signs, dominant element, and combination rarity percentage. Share it as an image or link — works without creating an account.

termsPage.s1P1: By accessing or using Estrevia ("Service", "we", "us"), you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree, do not use the Service.
termsPage.s1P2: We may update these Terms at any time. Continued use of the Service after changes constitutes acceptance of the revised Terms. We will provide notice of material changes via email or an in-app announcement at least 14 days before they take effect.
termsPage.s2P1: Estrevia is a progressive web application that calculates natal charts using sidereal astrology via Swiss Ephemeris, provides esoteric essays, planetary hours, and moon phase information, and generates shareable "Cosmic Passport" cards.
termsPage.s2P2: The Service is provided for personal, non-commercial use unless you hold a separate commercial license agreement with us.
termsPage.s3Headline: Astrology is for entertainment and personal reflection purposes only.
termsPage.s3P1: Nothing on Estrevia constitutes medical, psychological, financial, legal, or professional advice. Natal chart readings, planetary positions, and esoteric correspondences are not scientific claims and should not be used as a basis for important life decisions.
termsPage.s3P2: Always consult qualified professionals for medical, financial, or legal matters. Estrevia expressly disclaims any liability for decisions made based on astrological content provided through the Service.
termsPage.s4P1: You may use certain features without an account. Creating an account requires a valid email address. You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your credentials and for all activity under your account.
termsPage.s4P2: You must be at least 13 years old to create an account. By registering, you represent that you meet this age requirement.

termsPage.s10P1: These Terms are governed by applicable law. Any disputes shall be resolved through binding arbitration except where prohibited by law. Nothing in these Terms prevents you from exercising consumer protection rights under applicable law.

privacyPage.overviewP1: Estrevia is built on a privacy-first principle: your birth data is personal. We collect only what is necessary to provide the Service, encrypt sensitive data at rest, and give you full control over your information.
privacyPage.categoryBirthItem2: Time of birth (optional — required for house calculations)
privacyPage.categoryBirthNote: Encrypted with AES-256-GCM before storage. The encryption key lives in Vercel's secret environment — it never touches the database.
privacyPage.categoryAccountItem1: Email address (required to create an account)
privacyPage.categoryPassportItem1: Sun sign, Moon sign, Ascendant sign
privacyPage.categoryPassportNote: Share data contains derived astrological results only — never raw birth data.
privacyPage.categoryUsageNote: Collected only with your consent via cookie acceptance. Processed by PostHog (EU region).
privacyPage.categoryPaymentItem1: Billing information (card last 4 digits, expiry) — processed by Stripe
privacyPage.categoryPaymentNote: We never store full card numbers. All payment processing is handled by Stripe, Inc.
privacyPage.s2L1: Calculate and store your natal charts
privacyPage.s2L2: Generate and display your Cosmic Passport
privacyPage.s2L5: Send transactional emails (chart saved, subscription confirmation) via Resend
privacyPage.s2L6: Analyse product usage to improve the Service (with your consent)
privacyPage.s2L7: Monitor errors and performance via Sentry
privacyPage.s2NotSell: We do not sell your data, use it for advertising targeting, or share it with data brokers.
privacyPage.s3P1Before: Birth date, time, and location are classified as personal data under GDPR. We protect this data with
privacyPage.s3P1After: before it is written to the database.
privacyPage.s3L1: Each record uses a unique IV (initialisation vector)
privacyPage.s3L2: Encryption key stored in Vercel environment variables — not in the database
privacyPage.s3L3: Decryption happens only at request time, inside secure server functions
privacyPage.s3L4: Decrypted data is never logged or stored in intermediate systems
privacyPage.tpPosthogData: Anonymised usage events — only with cookie consent
privacyPage.tpVercelData: Request logs (IP, headers) — retained per Vercel policy
privacyPage.tpSentryData: Error stack traces, anonymised user context
privacyPage.s6Intro: If you are in the European Economic Area or the United Kingdom, you have the following rights regarding your personal data:
privacyPage.rightAccessDesc: Request a copy of all data we hold about you.
privacyPage.rightDeletionDesc: Delete your account and all associated data.
privacyPage.rightExportDesc: Download your data in machine-readable JSON.
privacyPage.rightObjectionDesc: Object to processing based on legitimate interests.

privacyPage.exerciseEmailPrefix: To exercise any other right, email
privacyPage.s7Intro: We use cookies and localStorage for:
privacyPage.s7L1Suffix: — Clerk session tokens. Necessary for the Service to function. No consent required.
privacyPage.s7L2Suffix: — PostHog client identifier (localStorage). Requires explicit consent via the cookie banner.
privacyPage.s7Footer: You can withdraw analytics consent at any time by clearing your browser's localStorage or contacting us. We do not use advertising cookies.
privacyPage.s8P1: Our analytics data (PostHog) is processed in the EU. Database infrastructure (Neon) may store data in the US. Where applicable, transfers are governed by Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) approved by the European Commission.
privacyPage.s9P1: Only users aged 13 or older may create an account. We do not knowingly collect personal data directly from children under 13.
privacyPage.s9P2Mid: An adult account holder may enter a child's birth data (date, time, location) to calculate a natal chart. In this case the adult acts as the data controller for that information, is responsible for obtaining any required parental consent, and may delete or export the data at any time via
privacyPage.s9P2After: . Birth data is stored encrypted with AES-256-GCM and is never used to profile the child, serve advertising, or train AI models.
privacyPage.s9P3Before: If you believe a child has created their own account or that personal data of a minor has been collected without proper consent, contact us at
privacyPage.s9P3After: and we will delete the data within 30 days.
privacyPage.s10P1: Estrevia operates as the data controller for personal data processed through the Service.
privacyPage.s10P2Prefix: Privacy enquiries, GDPR requests, and data breach reports:
privacyPage.s10P3: You also have the right to lodge a complaint with your local supervisory authority (e.g., ICO in the UK, CNIL in France).
